jokes.text: we've had to sell the science department skeleton and replace it with this Halloween costume
jokes.setup: School funding cuts requiring difficult decisions
jokes.punchline: Replacing real skeleton with cheap Halloween costume
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdist bureaucracy - specific, economical, and character-appropriate for Skinner

jokes.text: and your $1,500 deposits will not be refunded
jokes.setup: Already absurd Papa John's substitution
jokes.punchline: Still charging the same premium price
jokes.explanation: Perfect bureaucratic callousness - Skinner's matter-of-fact delivery of this injustice is character-perfect

jokes.text: Ooh, Papa John's!
jokes.setup: Announcement of terrible substitution
jokes.punchline: One parent is genuinely excited about Papa John's
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contrast - someone actually being pleased by the downgrade

jokes.text: Did you know that Hezbollah owns Little Dolly Snack Cakes?
jokes.setup: Homer explaining internet conspiracy theories
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous conspiracy connecting terrorist group to snack cakes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer conspiracy theory - specific, absurd, and delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Fine, I'll log onto Wahoo or Yippy or A-O-K or Pooka-dooka, whatever it's called
jokes.setup: Marge agreeing to get on the internet
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly silly made-up search engine names
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - showing her technological naivety through escalating silly names, very character-specific

jokes.text: I pay my taxes, I expect my orange drink!
jokes.setup: Parent wanting to move meeting along
jokes.punchline: Demanding government orange drink as taxpayer right
jokes.explanation: Decent civic entitlement joke - the specificity of 'orange drink' as a taxpayer demand is amusing

jokes.text: I never have to look out the window again
jokes.setup: Marge discovering weather websites
jokes.punchline: Deciding she'll never need to observe reality again
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's innocent over-enthusiasm for technology

jokes.text: Kids, get in the car! We're going to Pennsylvania!
jokes.setup: Marge finding increasingly cheap paper towel prices online
jokes.punchline: Willing to drive to another state to save pennies
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Marge's bargain-hunting obsession taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: And all this time, I thought 'Googling yourself' meant the other thing
jokes.setup: Homer discovering Google search results
jokes.punchline: Innuendo about masturbation
jokes.explanation: Fairly obvious sexual innuendo but delivered with Homer's characteristic cluelessness

jokes.text: Everyone can see you! Get inside! / Never! / Just put on a towel! / Why don't I just put on a dress?
jokes.setup: Marge discovering their house on satellite view
jokes.punchline: Homer's ridiculous gender-based objection to covering up
jokes.explanation: Great Homer masculinity joke - his absurd priorities about what constitutes emasculation

jokes.text: Not naked, I haven't
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer has met her parents
jokes.punchline: Homer's disturbing clarification about nudity
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character-specific disturbing logic from Homer

jokes.text: You're like Christopher Columbus. You discovered something millions of people knew about before you
jokes.setup: Lisa praising Marge for discovering the internet
jokes.punchline: Backhanded comparison highlighting how late Marge is to the party
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa snark - sophisticated historical comparison with perfect ironic twist, very quotable

jokes.text: The only thing that changes is the banner ad
jokes.setup: Marge repeatedly refreshing for new messages
jokes.punchline: Only the advertising changes, highlighting the futility
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about early internet experience and advertising ubiquity

jokes.text: Credit card information may be sold to Korean gangsters
jokes.setup: Standard terms and conditions agreement
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning identity theft to organized crime
jokes.explanation: Great parody of hidden terms in user agreements with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: Look at those bazooms. Who designed this character? Probably a man
jokes.setup: Marge seeing sexualized female game character
jokes.punchline: Astute observation about male game designers' priorities
jokes.explanation: Smart feminist commentary delivered in Marge's characteristically direct way

jokes.text: Body type: Olive Oyl
jokes.setup: Marge customizing her character to be more realistic
jokes.punchline: Choosing the famously thin Popeye character as body type
jokes.explanation: Good reference joke showing Marge's rejection of unrealistic body standards

jokes.text: it's like a renaissance fair, but without all the chubby couples
jokes.setup: Marge describing the fantasy game world
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about renaissance fair demographics
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with specific detail that rings true about renaissance fair culture

jokes.text: maybe I should run this by my husband first / Things are more fun if you just answer 'yes'
jokes.setup: Marge being cautious about game quest
jokes.punchline: Game encouraging impulsive behavior over marital consultation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's real-world caution applied to fantasy game

jokes.text: Hither me forth on mine arduous quest / Once again, just 'yes'
jokes.setup: Marge trying to speak in fantasy game language
jokes.punchline: NPC getting frustrated with her fake medieval speech
jokes.explanation: Funny subversion of RPG tropes with Marge's awkward attempt at medieval speak

jokes.text: We just got up. It's 7:00 a.m. / Actually, it's Saturday / I played a day and a night
jokes.setup: Kids being up late
jokes.punchline: Revealing Marge has been gaming for over 24 hours straight
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the addictive nature of gaming, particularly funny for Marge

jokes.text: The prison guards think I'm getting my online law degree
jokes.setup: Snake explaining how he accesses the game from prison
jokes.punchline: Deceiving guards about educational purposes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Snake line - criminal but clever, with good character integration

jokes.text: This game is a great way to meet eligible men who can afford a computer. Or have access to one at the school library
jokes.setup: Krabappel explaining online dating benefits
jokes.punchline: Lowering standards to include library computer users
jokes.explanation: Great Krabappel desperation humor with escalating lowered standards

jokes.text: It's amazing how you can be a turkey in every reality
jokes.setup: Observing another player's character
jokes.punchline: Insult suggesting they're losers in both virtual and real life
jokes.explanation: Excellent burn with philosophical undertones about virtual vs. real identity

jokes.text: I'm playing this while I'm on the can
jokes.setup: Moe joining the conversation
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about bathroom gaming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe overshare - gross but completely in character for his boundary issues

jokes.text: You're a troll. / What? No. My character's supposed to look like me. Why does everyone keep thinking I'm a troll?!
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Moe's character appearance
jokes.punchline: Moe's defensive reaction revealing his character actually does look like him
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moe self-roast - the implication that he naturally looks like a troll is perfectly brutal

jokes.text: He once beat me to death with my own life bar
jokes.setup: Describing the Shadow Knight's evil deeds
jokes.punchline: Impossible game mechanic turned into literal weapon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant gaming joke - turning UI element into weapon is creative and very quotable

jokes.text: He was a good man. A good, moist man
jokes.setup: Mourning a fallen player
jokes.punchline: Bizarre adjective choice of 'moist' for eulogy
jokes.explanation: Funny word choice - 'moist' is an inherently uncomfortable adjective for describing people

jokes.text: My son is an evil knight! The most successful evil knight in all the Earthland Realms! Not bad
jokes.setup: Marge discovering Bart is the feared Shadow Knight
jokes.punchline: Maternal pride in his virtual villainy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - finding the silver lining even in her son's virtual evil

jokes.text: I'm not a lady, it's a spell, a spell you said you'd reverse. / Yeah, yeah, it's on my list
jokes.setup: Milhouse complaining about being stuck as female character
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual dismissal of fixing serious problem
jokes.explanation: Good Bart/Milhouse dynamic showing Bart's casual cruelty to his best friend

jokes.text: Why Milhouse, don't you look pretty! / It's a spell! / And thank you
jokes.setup: Marge complimenting Milhouse's female avatar
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's conflicted response - upset but pleased by compliment
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Milhouse's desperate need for any positive attention

jokes.text: How would you like it if I suddenly started going shopping with you? / I'd like that very much
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about Marge invading his game
jokes.punchline: Marge genuinely wanting to spend more time together
jokes.explanation: Sweet reversal showing Marge's genuine desire for family bonding time

jokes.text: In the presence of a lady, a goblin must remove his hat and ears
jokes.setup: Continuing the Goblin's Code
jokes.punchline: Polite etiquette extended to removable body parts
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurdist etiquette - treating ears as removable like hats

jokes.text: You're making me look bad in front of my minions! / If they think less of you because of me, then they're not really your minions
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Marge's logical mom wisdom applied to evil minions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - applying maternal wisdom to fantasy villain context with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Here, let me enchant your pants
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to help Bart prepare for adventure
jokes.punchline: Maternal fussing translated into game mechanics
jokes.explanation: Good translation of maternal concern into fantasy game terms

jokes.text: In my day, girls were worried about their boobs
jokes.setup: Lisa being self-conscious about her shins
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate generational comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inappropriate comment delivered with characteristic cluelessness

jokes.text: Love counts for nothing! Business contacts are more important than love!
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on the movie Bend It Like Beckham
jokes.punchline: Homer enthusiastically supporting the wrong side's values
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment missing the point of the movie entirely

jokes.text: I always called it 'human foosball'
jokes.setup: Homer learning what soccer actually is
jokes.punchline: His reductive comparison to table game
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer comparison - reductive but actually quite accurate and memorable

jokes.text: Being a ref is a thankless job... the parents hate ya... the only upside is the kids share their snack with you
jokes.setup: Explaining why he quit refereeing
jokes.punchline: Pathetic perk of getting children's snacks
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about youth sports officiating with pathetic but believable detail

jokes.text: I got fired from Foot Locker. Kicks just keep getting harder to find...
jokes.setup: Explaining where he got referee outfit
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical reasoning about shoe difficulty
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer non sequitur - his explanation makes no logical sense

jokes.text: The American flag not good enough for ya? / That was my father. / I'm your father now
jokes.setup: Homer questioning player's patriotism
jokes.punchline: Declaring himself the child's new father
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's inappropriate authority, delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Until I swallowed the whistle
jokes.setup: Homer thinking the game went well
jokes.punchline: Revealing he accidentally ate his referee whistle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer physical comedy - completely plausible accident delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: You don't even know the rules! / - Hilarious joke? - Sadly, no
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Homer's refereeing
jokes.punchline: Homer's hopeful question and disappointed response
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with Homer hoping his incompetence was intentional comedy

jokes.text: I was about to go to the Pottery Barn site to get design ideas
jokes.setup: Waiting for something interesting to happen in game
jokes.punchline: Planning to browse furniture instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - even in fantasy games, she's thinking about home decoration

jokes.text: I'm in the tub right now
jokes.setup: Players suggesting meeting in real life
jokes.punchline: Moe's inappropriate overshare about current location
jokes.explanation: Good Moe callback to his bathroom gaming habits, maintaining his boundary-crossing character

jokes.text: I almost saw a goal! But there were so many ads for Spanish cell phones
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how he learned soccer rules
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by commercials during the rare exciting moment
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer observation - missing rare soccer excitement due to advertisement distraction

jokes.text: You are so blind, even Jesus couldn't heal you! / Helen, please. Don't drop the J bomb
jokes.setup: Helen making biblical insult
jokes.punchline: Reverend treating Jesus's name like profanity
jokes.explanation: Excellent religious satire - treating sacred name like swearing at children's soccer game

jokes.text: Lisa gets a penalty kick, and every other kid has to pay her a dollar
jokes.setup: Homer making referee call
jokes.punchline: Inventing bizarre financial penalty rule
jokes.explanation: Great Homer referee incompetence - making up rules that benefit his own daughter financially

jokes.text: Sir, I have sired a dum-dum, a mush-head, a what's-it, a dog boy, and something with a human face and fish body, what we called Kevin, but my young'uns is not dirty players
jokes.setup: Cletus defending his children from accusations
jokes.punchline: Listing increasingly bizarre offspring while defending their sportsmanship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Cletus escalation - the casual description of Kevin is comedy gold, perfectly in character

jokes.text: That's hill-William to you, sir
jokes.setup: Being called a hillbilly
jokes.punchline: Correcting to more dignified-sounding version
jokes.explanation: Excellent Cletus wordplay showing misguided attempt at dignity while maintaining character

jokes.text: You heard Geraldo
jokes.setup: Ronaldo explaining about flopping
jokes.punchline: Cletus mispronouncing the famous soccer player's name
jokes.explanation: Good Cletus mispronunciation showing his unfamiliarity with international soccer

jokes.text: When I put on these shorts, I'm not your father anymore, and judging by how tight they are, I'm never going to be anyone else's, either
jokes.setup: Homer explaining referee authority
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating joke about tight shorts affecting fertility
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer joke - good structure with unexpected fertility punchline, very quotable

jokes.text: Another family broken up by Ronaldo
jokes.setup: Lisa getting red card from her father
jokes.punchline: Ronaldo taking casual credit for family destruction
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Ronaldo treating family destruction as routine soccer consequence

jokes.text: I thought all the swords and severed heads were kind of gory, so I redecorated using the Hello Kitty expansion pack
jokes.setup: Bart discovering his trophy room changed
jokes.punchline: Marge's maternal sanitizing using cute Japanese characters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - applying maternal instincts to violent video game with hilariously inappropriate cute aesthetics

jokes.text: I was killed by my own son
jokes.setup: Marge explaining her game death
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overstatement of virtual matricide
jokes.explanation: Good Marge melodrama treating video game death as real family betrayal

jokes.text: A game I used to enjoy before you mommed all over it
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about the game changes
jokes.punchline: Using 'mom' as a verb for excessive maternal interference
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart neologism - 'mommed all over it' is creative and perfectly captures the concept

jokes.text: Son, I'll never understand women if I live to be 40. / Big if
jokes.setup: Homer expressing confusion about women
jokes.punchline: Bart doubting his father will reach 40
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy - Bart's casual dismissal of Homer's life expectancy

jokes.text: He, Moe, you give good advice. / Yeah. What have you done with the real Moe?
jokes.setup: Moe giving unusually helpful advice
jokes.punchline: Questioning if this is actually Moe due to helpfulness
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke - acknowledging Moe's usual unhelpfulness makes wisdom suspicious

jokes.text: 22 years later, this riot is still going on
jokes.setup: Documentary about soccer violence
jokes.punchline: Absurdly long-lasting riot duration
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of soccer violence to impossible duration

jokes.text: a statue of the Virgin Mary came alive... and beat the holy snot out of everyone
jokes.setup: Describing violent soccer riot
jokes.punchline: Religious statue engaging in violence with religious pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent religious satire with great wordplay - 'holy snot' is inspired pun in this context

jokes.text: Now I'm paying for crap like American Masters
jokes.setup: Homer becoming PBS subscriber to get DVD
jokes.punchline: Complaining about quality educational programming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - calling prestigious documentary series 'crap' shows his cultural priorities

jokes.text: Those places are pretty terrible
jokes.setup: Discussion of Europe and South America being made insane by soccer
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact agreement about entire continents
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan from Lisa casually dismissing multiple continents as terrible

jokes.text: If you kill me, I'll egg your houses in real life! / It's still worth it / I live in a loft!
jokes.setup: Player threatening real-world revenge
jokes.punchline: Clarification making threat less effective due to housing situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about real-world consequences undermined by living situation

jokes.text: Whatever. I'm gonna go play outside
jokes.setup: Marge promising to hunt down his virtual killers
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual dismissal to go do normal kid activities
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - kid choosing real world over protective parent's virtual obsession

jokes.text: Why am I paying $14.95 a month for this?
jokes.setup: Marge alone in the empty game world
jokes.punchline: Questioning the value of subscription gaming
jokes.explanation: Good ending moment showing Marge's practical nature reasserting after gaming obsession
